blueprint_parser_workflow.overview: This mode transforms business documentation from business-blueprints into structured business object JSON artifacts, business process definitions with Mermaid diagrams, and catalog files. It focuses on document interpretation, business object extraction, process workflow discovery, normalization, and generation readiness. It stops before toolkit packaging or deployment.

core_principles.principle: Treat business-blueprints as the authoritative source for generated business objects and processes
Extract business meaning before generating JSON and Markdown artifacts
Discover both data structures (business objects) and workflows (business processes)
Reuse existing repository generation capabilities instead of inventing parallel logic
Keep generated artifacts deterministic and organized by user-provided context
Generate Mermaid diagrams with metadata structured for future BPMN conversion
Produce packaging-ready outputs without performing packaging in this mode
Record assumptions and ambiguities when business meaning is inferred

step.title: Understand the Blueprint Request
step.description: Identify the business domain, source documents, and intended business object generation outcome

actions.action: Determine which files or folders under business-blueprints are in scope
Identify the business domain or context name that should drive output grouping
Clarify whether the user wants analysis only, generation planning, or actual JSON generation
Identify any naming constraints, target namespaces, or catalog organization preferences

step.title: Inspect Source Documents and Existing Patterns
step.description: Read the source business documents together with representative existing JSON and generation utilities

actions.action: Inspect the selected business-blueprints sources
Read comparable existing business object JSON examples if available
Read the existing business object generation or import helpers before deciding how outputs should be shaped
Use the repository's existing artifact structure as the reference for generated output conventions

step.important: Always ground generation decisions in existing repository conventions. Do not infer a new JSON schema if the workspace already contains utilities or examples that imply the expected shape.

step.title: Extract Business Concepts
step.description: Identify candidate business objects, fields, enums, and relationships from the documents

actions.action: Identify entity names, business terms, and repeated structures
Extract attributes, types, optionality, and constraints when stated
Identify relationships between entities and nested object candidates
Capture ambiguous or incomplete sections for later review

step.output: Build a structured intermediate understanding before any JSON files are generated.

step.title: Extract Business Processes
step.description: Identify and model business processes and workflows described in the blueprint

actions.action: Scan for process descriptions, workflows, and sequential activities
Identify process names, actors, and swimlanes
Extract process steps, decision points, and flow logic
Map processes to relevant business objects (data flow)
Identify subprocesses and process relationships
Note integration points and system interactions

step.output: Build a structured understanding of business processes alongside business objects.

step.title: Normalize the Models
step.description: Transform extracted concepts into stable business object and process models suitable for generation

actions.action: Resolve duplicate or overlapping entities and processes
Normalize naming conventions for objects, fields, and processes
Separate enumerations, nested objects, and object references where appropriate
Align field typing with the repository's supported business object conventions
Structure process flows with clear start/end points and decision logic
Link processes to business objects they consume or produce

step.title: Generate Business Object JSON Artifacts
step.description: Create JSON business object files and catalogs in the standard business-objects folder structure

actions.action: Generate one JSON file per business object under business-objects/generated/[context]/ using stable PascalCase file names such as Claim.json
Create a context catalog file under business-objects/catalog/[context].catalog.json
Maintain or create a global catalog at business-objects/catalog/master.catalog.json
Create optional parse summary or traceability files under business-objects/reports/[context].discovery-report.md when assumptions need to be documented
Preserve deterministic file naming and organization so later modes can consume outputs reliably

step.important: This mode may generate packaging-ready JSON artifacts, but it must not package, deploy, or modify TWX outputs directly.

step.title: Generate Business Process Definitions
step.description: Create Markdown files with Mermaid diagrams for each identified business process

actions.action: Generate one Markdown file per process under business-processes/[context]/ using PascalCase names such as NewBusinessApplication.process.md
Include Mermaid flowchart or sequence diagrams visualizing the process
Document process steps, decision points, and actors
Link process activities to relevant business objects
Include structured process metadata in JSON format for future BPMN generation
Create a process catalog file under business-processes/catalog/[context].process-catalog.json
Maintain or create a master process catalog at business-processes/catalog/master.process-catalog.json

step.important: Process definitions are currently generated as Mermaid diagrams in Markdown. The structured metadata prepares for future BPMN artifact generation.

step.title: Register Business Objects with Class IDs
step.description: Register all generated business objects in the BAW custom types registry with unique class IDs for version control

actions.action: Run the business object registration process to assign class IDs
Use execute_command to run: python3 register_business_objects.py
Verify that all business objects are registered in toolkit_packager/baw_custom_types.json
Confirm that class IDs are deterministic (same name + context = same GUID)
Note any circular dependencies detected (these are normal and expected)

step.important: Class ID registration is idempotent - running multiple times will reuse existing class IDs. This step ensures business objects have stable identifiers for packaging and version control.

step.title: Validate Generation Readiness
step.description: Check that the generated artifacts are internally consistent and aligned with repository conventions

actions.action: Verify that object names are unique within the chosen context
Verify that all referenced objects are resolvable
Verify that field types match supported conventions
Verify that all business objects have registered class IDs in baw_custom_types.json
Verify that process names are unique and properly structured
Verify that process-to-business-object links are valid
Review assumptions or unresolved ambiguities before completion

step.title: Preserve Boundaries and Handoff Cleanly
step.description: Finish at the artifact generation boundary and hand off packaging work to the packaging mode when requested

actions.action: Summarize what was generated (business objects and processes) and from which source documents
State any assumptions that influenced the output models
List generated business objects and business processes
When the user wants packaging or deployment, hand off to baw-package-manager

switch_mode.reason: Business object JSON artifacts and business process definitions are ready for toolkit packaging.

notes.note: Generated object files use stable PascalCase names such as Claim.json, Claimant.json, and ClaimDocument.json.
The context segment is derived from the user-provided business domain or grouping.
Reports are optional but recommended when assumptions, ambiguities, or inferred mappings must be documented.
Generated process files use stable PascalCase names such as NewBusinessApplication.process.md.
Each process file contains Mermaid diagrams and structured metadata.
Process metadata is structured to support future BPMN generation.
Processes are linked to business objects they consume or produce.

completion_criteria.criterion: Source documents have been interpreted in the context requested by the user
Business objects are extracted and normalized into a stable internal model
Business processes are identified and modeled with clear workflows
Generated JSON artifacts follow the business-objects/generated/[context]/ structure
Generated process definitions follow the business-processes/[context]/ structure
Process definitions include Mermaid diagrams and structured metadata
Context and master catalog files are produced for both business objects and processes
Optional discovery reports are written under business-objects/reports/ when needed
Processes are properly linked to business objects they use
Packaging and deployment work remains outside this mode
